negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing RETH_MAX_TRANSACTIONS_PER_BLOCK cap across heartbeat, scheduled, async commitment, settlement pair, delivery, and regular inclusion paths.
related_but_insufficient_code: The builder increments executed_payload_candidate_count for several inclusion paths and enforces gas/block-size limits, but no transaction-count cap environment variable or pair-slot accounting was found.

rationale: The transaction-count cap candidate covers all named inclusion paths and the bounded packet has no later contradiction.
statement: When RETH_MAX_TRANSACTIONS_PER_BLOCK is set to a valid number, payload building stops adding transactions once the included transaction count reaches the cap across heartbeat, async commitment, scheduled, unified, and regular inclusion paths; async commitment plus settlement pairs are skipped unless both transactions fit.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set RETH_MAX_TRANSACTIONS_PER_BLOCK to a small value, offer heartbeat, scheduled, regular, async commitment, and async settlement pair candidates, and assert the payload never exceeds the cap and never includes only one half of a pair when one slot remains.
summary: Payload builder transaction-count cap is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set RETH_MAX_TRANSACTIONS_PER_BLOCK=1 and offer a two-transaction async settlement pair; current builder has no count cap to prevent overrun or partial-pair admission.
title: Payload builder enforces a block transaction-count cap
